title: Notification model has duplicate/misplaced @@index after @@map
description: Lines 805-808 declare indexes for Notification, but line 807 closes the model with `@@map("notifications")` BEFORE the final `@@index([userId, read, createdAt])` on line 808. The trailing `@@index` is positioned after the `@@map` directive, breaking the model block. Prisma accepts this, but the more serious issue is that lines 805 and 808 are partially redundant (805 covers `[userId, read]`, 808 covers `[userId, read, createdAt]`) — the composite index on line 808 supersedes line 805 for any read+sort query, so line 805 is dead weight. Also, 808 is the form PostgreSQL's planner will pick for unread-count queries, so 805 is unnecessary.
suggested_fix: Reorder so the `@@map("notifications")` and `@@index` directives are all grouped before any later model declaration. Drop the redundant `@@index([userId, read])` (line 805) since `@@index([userId, read, createdAt])` (line 808) covers the same prefix. Also add `@@index([userId, createdAt])` is already present on line 806 — keep it.

title: Webhook model missing @@map and Webhook.project FK has no onDelete
description: The Webhook model declares a `projectId String?` field and a `project Project?` relation, but the relation is defined as `project Project? @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id])` — no `@@map`, no `onDelete: Cascade`, no `@@index([projectId])`. When a project is hard-deleted (see hard-delete.ts), any webhooks bound to it become dangling pointers. Likewise, the Webhook has no `@@map("webhooks")` (compare WebhookDelivery, which has no `@@map` either on line 1075).
suggested_fix: Add `@@map("webhooks")` on the Webhook model, `@@map("webhook_deliveries")` on WebhookDelivery, `@@index([projectId])` on Webhook, and change the relation to `onDelete: SetNull` so a project hard-delete nullifies the FK rather than leaving it dangling.

title: WebhookDelivery.payload should be @db.Text
description: `payload String` (line 1067) is documented as a JSON string for delivery. Webhook payloads can be tens of KB (work_package + nested relations, etc.) — using Postgres' default `varchar(255)` is far too small and will fail at runtime. Also, `responseBody String?` (line 1070) has the same problem if a remote server returns a long error page.
suggested_fix: Add `@db.Text` to both `payload` and `responseBody` columns. Also add `@@index([webhookId, status])` to speed up retry/queue queries in lib/webhooks/dispatcher.ts.

title: EmailQueue has no FK to User and no userId column
description: `lib/email/index.ts:56` creates rows in emailQueue with `{ to, subject, html, scheduledAt }` only — no userId. The result is orphan rows with no way to trace who the email was meant for, no cascade when a user is deleted, and no way to deduplicate 'has this user been notified' queries. The cron worker (pages/api/cron/process-email-queue.ts) can't filter by user either.
suggested_fix: Add `userId String?` and `user User? @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: SetNull)` to EmailQueue; add `userEmails User[]` reverse relation on User. Backfill in code: derive `userId` from session/recipient in lib/email/index.ts and `prisma.emailQueue.create`.

title: WikiPageVersion.authorId set to original page author, not the editor
description: On PATCH (line 65-92), the new WikiPageVersion is created with `authorId: currentPage.authorId` (line 86) — i.e. the user who originally created the page — not `session.user.id` (the user who is currently editing). Same bug at pages/api/wiki/[id]/restore.ts:59. Audit trails of who edited what are now permanently wrong.
suggested_fix: Replace `authorId: currentPage.authorId` with `authorId: session.user.id` in both files. Also add a unit test asserting that PATCH and restore set authorId to the calling session user.

title: User.totpSecret / backupCodes / webAuthnCreds / secondFactorPending should be @db.Text
description: Lines 71-76 store TOTP secrets, backup-code arrays, and WebAuthn credentials as JSON strings in a default `String` column. With Prisma's default mapping to `varchar(191)` on PostgreSQL (or the datasource default), these JSON blobs can overflow for users with several WebAuthn credentials. The same problem exists for `apiKey` on line 79 (although that's typically a short token, defense-in-depth).
suggested_fix: Add `@db.Text` to `totpSecret`, `backupCodes`, `webAuthnCreds`, `secondFactorPending`, and `apiKey`. Consider adding length constants in lib/2fa/* and validating before write.

title: BudgetLine.workPackageId has no onDelete (dangling FK risk)
description: BudgetLine (line 371) has `workPackageId String?` and `workPackage WorkPackage? @relation(fields: [workPackageId], references: [id])` — no onDelete action. When a WorkPackage is deleted (line 274-276 in work-packages/[id].ts), the BudgetLine's workPackageId becomes a dangling FK. Postgres' default RESTRICT would also cause the delete to fail, which may be the only thing keeping it consistent today. Either way, the schema doesn't declare intent.
suggested_fix: Add `onDelete: SetNull` to match the soft-delete semantics of budgets (budget lines should survive WP deletion). Also add `@@index([workPackageId])` to speed up the budget→WP join used in pages/api/projects/[projectId]/budgets/[id]/report.ts.

title: SprintMember has no User back-relation and no onDelete on User
description: SprintMember declares `userId String` (line 935) but the model has NO `user User @relation(...)` field, so Prisma has no back-relation from User → SprintMember. Additionally, the User → SprintMember cascade isn't defined. If a User is hard-deleted (pages/api/users/[id]/2fa/* + GDPR path), SprintMember rows stay orphaned. Also: SprintMember has no User-side `sprintMemberships SprintMember[]` relation, blocking eager-include of user avatars in sprint board views.
suggested_fix: Add `user User @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)` to SprintMember and `sprintMemberships SprintMember[]` to User. Also drop the now-implicit `onDelete: Cascade` on the sprintId FK (line 938) if desired — it's already correct.

title: Project.generatedFrom is a string, not a self-FK
description: Line 138 declares `generatedFrom String?` with no `Project?` relation or `onDelete: SetNull`. This is a 'soft' FK to projects.id — there's no referential integrity, no way to Prisma-include the source project, and no cascade when the template is deleted. Renaming/deleting a template orphanes generatedFrom strings silently.
suggested_fix: Replace with a proper self-relation: `template Project? @relation("TemplateFrom", fields: [generatedFrom], references: [id], onDelete: SetNull)` and add the reverse `generatedProjects Project[] @relation("TemplateFrom")` on Project.

title: SavedQuery and Query are duplicate models
description: SavedQuery (line 299) and Query (line 447) have nearly identical columns (userId, projectId, name, filters, sortBy, groupBy, displayMode, isDefault, createdAt, updatedAt) and the same purpose: persisted view filters per user. Having two models causes client/server confusion (`prisma.savedQuery.findMany` vs `prisma.query.findMany` — which is canonical?). One is dead code or both diverge soon.
suggested_fix: Pick one (recommend `Query` since it has the cleaner name and the explicit `filters/sortBy` JSON types) and delete the other. Migrate any seed/import scripts that reference the removed model.

title: pg Pool is created per cold-start with no max/connection limits and not cached globally
description: `createPrismaClient` (line 10) instantiates `new Pool({ connectionString })` with no `max`, `idleTimeoutMillis`, or `connectionTimeoutMillis` options. The `globalForPrisma.pool` slot (line 7) is declared but never assigned, so serverless cold starts open a new pool every cold start. Under Vercel/Edge load this is wasteful and can exhaust Postgres connection limits.
suggested_fix: Cache the pool globally like the PrismaClient: `const pool = globalForPrisma.pool ?? new Pool({ connectionString, max: 10, idleTimeoutMillis: 30_000 });`. In non-prod, assign back to `globalForPrisma.pool`. Also pass `pgPool` options (`max`, `connectionTimeoutMillis`) to avoid runaway connections.

title: TimeEntry soft-delete inconsistent: filtered only by status string, no @@index on deletedAt
description: `TimeEntry` declares `deletedAt DateTime?` (line 336) and the comment on line 335 says 'historical entries cannot be deleted' but the time-reports endpoints (time-reports/by-user.ts:88, by-project.ts:88) iterate over all entries without filtering `deletedAt: null`. The schema has `@@index([workPackageId, spentOn])` and `@@index([userId, spentOn])` but no `@@index([deletedAt])` or composite `@@index([userId, spentOn, deletedAt])` — meaning every aggregation in time-reports will scan soft-deleted rows. Same applies to Document (line 671) which also has `deletedAt` but no soft-delete-aware index.
suggested_fix: Add `@@index([deletedAt])` to TimeEntry and Document. Update time-reports endpoints to filter `deletedAt: null` in the where clause, and verify Document GET routes do the same (pages/api/projects/[projectId]/documents/index.ts).

title: WorkPackage PATCH runs 3+ writes without a transaction
description: Lines 127-144 do `findUnique` → `update`, then lines 166-211 do `activity.create` → `emitActivity` → `notification.create` → `broadcastNotification` → `broadcastWorkPackageUpdate` — five sequential writes outside any transaction. If `emitActivity` fails halfway, the WP is updated but no activity row is created; the assignee is told about a state change that didn't get logged. There is also a stale-write race: between the `old` fetch (line 127) and the update (line 129), a concurrent PATCH can mutate the row, and we report stale diff details to the activity log.
suggested_fix: Wrap `findUnique` + `update` + `activity.create` (the polymorphic one — see DB-14) inside `prisma.$transaction`. Move `emitActivity` and `notification.create` inside the same transaction; only the SSE broadcasts should be fire-and-forget after commit.

title: activity.create uses non-existent field 'workPackageId'
description: Line 167-174 calls `prisma.activity.create({ data: { workPackageId: id, userId: body.authorId, action: 'updated', details: ... } })` — but the Activity model is polymorphic (subjectType/subjectId, see schema.prisma:392-418). There is NO `workPackageId` column on Activity. This code path would throw `Unknown argument 'workPackageId'` at runtime. The very next block (line 177) correctly calls `emitActivity` (the polymorphic helper), so the broken `prisma.activity.create` is duplicated dead/broken code. Same problem in pages/api/work-packages/index.ts:190-197 (POST work-package) and pages/api/work-packages/[id].ts:255-291 (DELETE) — wait, DELETE correctly uses emitActivity, so only the PATCH and POST have the broken twin call.
suggested_fix: Delete the broken `prisma.activity.create({ data: { workPackageId, userId, action, details } })` calls in pages/api/work-packages/[id].ts:167-174 (PATCH) and pages/api/work-packages/index.ts:190-197 (POST). The `emitActivity` helper on the next lines is the correct API.

title: Member has no @@index on roleId (high-traffic JOIN column)
description: Line 155 declares `role Role @relation(fields: [roleId], references: [id])` but no `@@index([roleId])`. The permissions check in lib/auth/project.ts and the role-permission cache in lib/permissions/check.ts both do `member.findUnique({ where: { userId_projectId }, include: { role: { select: { permissions: true } } } })`. The `role` join is fast via the implicit FK, but a `roleId`-only query (e.g. listing all members with a given role) would full-scan the members table. Also, the unique `@@unique([userId, projectId])` (line 157) is fine but the inverse `[projectId, userId]` is not indexed — most queries by project will be unindexed.
suggested_fix: Add `@@index([roleId])` and `@@index([projectId])` to Member. The unique on `[userId, projectId]` is fine but consider whether the read pattern is more often `WHERE projectId = ?` (the dashboard list view) — if so, a non-unique index on `projectId` is worth adding even with the existing composite.

title: WorkPackageRelation has no @@index, no @@map, no self-relation prevent
description: Lines 283-293: WorkPackageRelation has `fromId` and `toId` (both FKs to WorkPackage, both onDelete: Cascade) but no `@@index([fromId])` or `@@index([toId])`. The `pages/api/work-packages/[id]/relations.ts` GET does a join through both columns, and pages/api/relations/[id].ts deletes by id. Without indexes, every relation-list query on a work package scans the entire relations table. Also missing `@@map("work_package_relations")` (well, it has that on line 292 — good) — but missing `@@unique([fromId, toId, relationType])` would allow accidental duplicate relations.
suggested_fix: Add `@@index([fromId])`, `@@index([toId])`, and `@@unique([fromId, toId, relationType])` to prevent the same relation type being recorded twice. Also add an enum for `relationType` (currently free string: 'blocks'/'blocked_by'/'precedes'/'follows'/'relates') so typos are caught at the DB layer.

title: WikiPage has no soft-delete and WikiPageVersion uniqueness is by version only
description: WikiPage (line 512) is hard-deletable (line 113 of wiki/[id].ts: `prisma.wikiPage.delete`) with no soft-delete mirror like Document (which has `deletedAt` on line 671). This is inconsistent — Documents preserve history via soft-delete, Wikis don't. Also: WikiPageVersion has `@@index([wikiPageId])` but no `@@unique([wikiPageId, version])` — two parallel transactions could both insert version 5 if they fetched `version+1` simultaneously. The check in pages/api/wiki/[id].ts:43 only reads the current page; a concurrent PATCH could pass the same `newVersion` and both writes succeed, creating two 'version 5' rows.
suggested_fix: Add `@@unique([wikiPageId, version])` to WikiPageVersion, mirroring DocumentVersion's pattern (line 699). Add a soft-delete column (`deletedAt DateTime?`) to WikiPage for parity with Document. Also add `@@index([projectId])` to WikiPage — most queries are by project.

title: Query/SavedQuery.filters has no validation, accepts any JSON
description: Query (line 452) stores `filters Json` and `sortBy Json` as opaque JSON. Without a server-side zod check before write, a client could store malformed filter shapes that crash the read path. Work-package routes must validate JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(...))-shaped data on read. The same risk applies to Activity.details (line 400).
suggested_fix: Add a `z.WorkPackageFilter` schema in lib/permissions/work-packages.ts and a `parseFilters` helper that runs on every read. Store filters as the canonical schema, not the raw JSON payload. Add a `formatVersion` field (e.g. 2) so future migrations can re-parse stored data.

title: MeetingAttendee.response is free string, not enum
description: Line 747 declares `response String @default("none") // "none", "accepted", "declined"`. The comment lists 3 values but the schema allows arbitrary strings, and pages/api/projects/[projectId]/meetings/[id]/rsvp/index.ts:53 maps the API status to one of these three by hand. A typo (`'accept'`) silently becomes a valid DB row, breaking the upcoming-meetings filter and the dashboard counts.
suggested_fix: Replace with an enum: `enum MeetingResponse { NONE ACCEPTED DECLINED TENTATIVE }` and `response MeetingResponse @default(NONE)`. Same enum used by the rsvp endpoint mapper.

title: ProjectModule.module is free string, should be enum
description: Line 499: `module String // 'work_packages', 'gantt', ...` is a fixed list of module names, but accepts any string. With no enum, you can register a 'foo' module and the frontend silently ignores it.
suggested_fix: Add `enum ProjectModuleName { WORK_PACKAGES GANTT BOARD CALENDAR WIKI FORUMS DOCUMENTS MEETINGS TIME_TRACKING }` and change `module String` → `module ProjectModuleName`.

title: MyPageLayout.widgets is opaque JSON with no schema version
description: Line 1189 stores `widgets Json` without a format-version field. As the WidgetType union in types/my-page.ts evolves, stored layouts from older app versions will fail to render. The current 'replaces the pre-existing static-defaults stub' comment shows this is exactly the kind of code that mutates over time. There's also no @@map and no index on userId beyond the unique.
suggested_fix: Add `formatVersion Int @default(1)` and a `parseWidgets()` helper in hooks/use-my-page.ts that migrations old shapes. Add `@@index([userId])` to surface the unique-key status. Consider adding a one-to-many `MyPageWidget` table for queryability, though that would change the API contract significantly.

title: Identifier uniqueness check is a non-transactional while-loop
description: Lines 75-78: `while (await prisma.project.findUnique({ where: { identifier: newIdentifier } }))` is a TOCTOU race. Two concurrent copy-project requests for the same source project will both pass the check, then both attempt `prisma.project.create` with the same identifier — one wins, the other gets a P2002 unique-constraint error (which isn't caught — the route returns 500 instead of 409).
suggested_fix: Either (a) catch P2002 in the catch and retry with `-counter+1`, or (b) rely on the unique constraint and let Postgres enforce it. Best: pre-fetch a max counter in one query (`SELECT MAX(suffix) FROM projects WHERE identifier LIKE 'base-%'`) and use that as the start, accepting that a concurrent collision is caught by the constraint.

title: Wiki page mutation (PATCH) not using withRoute HOF and not in transaction with version creation
description: Note: actual file is pages/api/projects/[projectId]/wiki/[slug].ts. The wiki PATCH path is the same file as wiki/[id].ts above; flagged separately for the file-path-level concern. More importantly, both PATCH paths (wiki/[id].ts and wiki/[id]/restore.ts) compute `newVersion = currentPage.version + 1` outside the transaction. If two PATCHes race, both will write the same version, creating two 'version N' rows (DB-17's symptom). The current `WikiPageVersion` schema has no unique constraint to prevent this.
suggested_fix: Move the version increment inside the transaction, use `tx.wikiPage.update({ where: { id }, data: { version: { increment: 1 }, ... } })` so Postgres atomically increments and returns the new value, then insert the version row using that returned value. Combined with DB-17's `@@unique([wikiPageId, version])`, this prevents both the version-collision race and the duplicate-version-row race.

title: Announcement has no authorId / no audit trail
description: Announcement (line 1081) is a system-wide banner but has no `authorId` — `pages/api/announcements/index.ts` creates them without recording who. Pages/api/admin/branding (admin-only) and announcements/dismiss.ts both touch the table, but no audit row exists.
suggested_fix: Add `authorId String` and `author User @relation(fields: [authorId], references: [id])` to Announcement. Also add `@@map("announcements")` (currently missing) and `@@index([startsAt, endsAt])` to speed up the 'currently active' lookup on every page load.

title: Version model has no @@index on projectId
description: Line 469-475: `Version` (used for milestones) has `projectId` FK with `onDelete: Cascade` but no `@@index([projectId])`. The `pages/api/projects/[projectId]/sprints/[sprintId]/board/index.ts` and similar endpoints will scan the versions table on every project. Also missing `@@index([dueDate])` for the roadmap/sort-by-due queries.
suggested_fix: Add `@@index([projectId])`, `@@index([dueDate])`, and a unique constraint `@@unique([projectId, name])` to prevent duplicate version names per project.

title: Project.moduleProjectModule composite doesn't have a reverse from ProjectModule perspective
description: Wait — ProjectModule is on the project. Project side is fine (line 106). But the User model (lines 30-83) has no `createdProjects Project[] @relation("ProjectCreator")` — there's no way to know who created a project, and `Project` has no `createdById` field. Audit/compliance queries (who created project X) are impossible.
suggested_fix: Add `createdById String?`, `creator User? @relation("ProjectCreator", fields: [createdById], references: [id], onDelete: SetNull)`, and `createdProjects Project[] @relation("ProjectCreator")` on User. Backfill `createdById` from the session in projects POST.

title: DocumentFolder.children has no onDelete for parent self-relation
description: DocumentFolder (line 704) has `parentId String?` and `parent DocumentFolder? @relation("DocumentFolderHierarchy", fields: [parentId], references: [id])` but no `onDelete`. The DB default RESTRICT would prevent deleting a folder with subfolders, which is correct intent, but the schema doesn't declare it. Also, Document.folderId (line 661) has no onDelete either — deleting a folder orphanes documents.
suggested_fix: Add `onDelete: Restrict` to DocumentFolder.parent self-relation, and `onDelete: SetNull` to Document.folder (mirroring WorkPackage.sprint pattern at line 217). Or `onDelete: Cascade` on the folder relation if deletion of folder implies deletion of contained documents (with explicit confirmation).

title: WorkPackageRelation has no relation-type validation
description: Line 287: `relationType String // "blocks", "blocked_by", "precedes", "follows", "relates"` is a free string. Same as DB-19/20: typos like `'block'` are silently accepted, breaking the relations graph renderer.
suggested_fix: Add `enum RelationType { BLOCKS BLOCKED_BY PRECEDES FOLLOWS RELATES }` and `relationType RelationType`. Update the relations API routes to use the enum.

title: User.isSystemAdmin and isAPIUser mixed with regular user fields
description: Lines 23-25 add flags directly on User. The apiKey on line 79 is concerning — there's no rotation, no expiry, no scope. Combined with `isSystemAdmin` being a single boolean, compromise of any user with API key + isSystemAdmin=true is total. There's also no audit log for who has had admin promoted.
suggested_fix: Move API key to a separate `ApiKey` model with `userId`, `scopes String[]`, `lastUsedAt DateTime?`, `expiresAt DateTime?`, `revokedAt DateTime?`. Keep `isSystemAdmin` on User (it's still useful) but add an `AdminGrant` audit table for promotions/demotions. Add `@@index([userId])` and `@@index([expiresAt])` on ApiKey.

title: No @db.Text on Query/Activity.filters, Activity.details, NotificationSetting.notificationType
description: Multiple JSON-shaped columns (Query.filters line 452, Query.sortBy line 453, Activity.details line 400, Activity.reference line 404) are stored as default `Json` (which on Postgres becomes `jsonb` — fine for size, but `Json` on Prisma 7+ is `jsonb` by default). NotificationSetting.notificationType (line 816) is a free string. Verify via `npx prisma db pull` that the actual PG types match. Also: NotificationSetting.notificationType should be an enum (see DB-19/20/28).
suggested_fix: After running `prisma migrate dev`, run `\d+ activities` to confirm the columns are jsonb. If any are `text`, add `@db.JsonB` explicitly. Replace notificationType with `enum NotificationType` covering all 5+ types mentioned in code.

title: group add-member has no transaction and no rate-limit
description: Lines 64-95: addMember does `findUnique group` → `findUnique user` → `create` as three separate writes with no transaction. If the user is hard-deleted between the existence check and the create, you get a 500 instead of a clean 404. Also: no rate limit (the function is admin-only but a buggy admin can spam adds). No soft-delete or audit trail.
suggested_fix: Wrap the existence checks and the create in `prisma.$transaction`. Consider using `prisma.groupMembership.upsert` (with the unique constraint `[groupId, userId]`) to avoid the existence-check race entirely. Add `checkRateLimit` call for admin mutating routes. Emit a unified activity row for compliance.

title: Notification has no @@index on (projectId, createdAt) for per-project feeds
description: Lines 805-808: only `userId`-keyed indexes. If a future feature shows 'notifications fired in project X' (audit view), the query `WHERE projectId = ? ORDER BY createdAt DESC` will full-scan. The `lib/notifications/realtime.ts` SSE broadcast also projects-by-id, so a per-project index is increasingly likely.
suggested_fix: Add `@@index([projectId, createdAt])`. Also add `@@index([userId, reason])` for the unsubscribe-by-reason flow that NotificationSetting enables.

title: Project model is missing @@index on identifier (unique) — duplicate identifier check races
description: Line 96 declares `identifier String @unique` — the unique constraint creates a unique B-tree, so lookups by identifier are fine. BUT the same identifier can be created concurrently in two project creation requests: `pages/api/projects/index.ts` likely does a findUnique check then create (TOCTOU).
suggested_fix: Audit pages/api/projects/index.ts: if it does `findUnique` → `create` for identifier uniqueness, switch to `create` and catch P2002. This is the same pattern as DB-22 in copy.ts.

title: Invite model allows duplicate pending invites for same email + project
description: Invite (line 1136) has `email String`, `projectId String`, and three single-column indexes (lines 1153-1155) but no `@@unique([projectId, email, acceptedAt IS NULL])` partial-unique. A user can be invited 5 times to the same project with 5 active tokens. pages/api/projects/[projectId]/members/invite/index.ts probably does an existence check then create — same TOCTOU pattern as DB-22/33.
suggested_fix: Add a partial unique index in a raw migration: `CREATE UNIQUE INDEX invite_pending_unique ON invites (projectId, email) WHERE "acceptedAt" IS NULL;` (Prisma can't express partial-unique directly; use `@@index` + a follow-up raw migration). Alternatively, add `@@unique([projectId, email])` and accept that 're-invite after accept' is allowed but 're-invite while pending' is rejected.

title: MyPageLayout has no @@index on userId (only the unique on userId)
description: The unique on userId already creates an index, but `MyPageLayout` has no `@@index` directives and no `@@map` (line 1195 has it). The bigger concern is the no-tests coverage gap: pages/api/my-page/index.ts exists but is unverified — this is a Phase 5 Sprint 2 fix per the comment.
suggested_fix: Add `@@index([userId])` for explicitness (the unique constraint already provides this; the explicit declaration is documentation). Add a unit test under __tests__/api/my-page.unit.test.ts covering: GET default fallback, GET persisted layout, POST/PATCH upsert, RBAC.

title: WikiPage missing @@index on authorId
description: Lines 512-532: WikiPage has `authorId` (line 519) but no `@@index([authorId])`. Pages like 'pages by author X' would full-scan. Low priority unless that view exists, but the column is high-cardinality and used in 'recently edited by me' patterns.
suggested_fix: Add `@@index([authorId])`. Also add `@@index([parentId])` for the children-of-page traversal in pages/api/projects/[projectId]/wiki/index.ts.

title: Query has no userId FK / relation back to User
description: Query (line 447) declares `userId String` with NO `user User @relation(...)` and no onDelete. If a user is hard-deleted, their saved queries orphan. The polymorphic subject is also a free string — same enum-needed issue as DB-19/20/28.
suggested_fix: Add `user User @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)` to Query, plus the reverse `queries Query[]` on User. Same for SavedQuery if it survives DB-10's dedup.

title: User has no @@index on email beyond @unique
description: Line 18 declares `email String @unique` — the unique constraint provides an index. The concern: lookup-by-email is in the hot path of NextAuth (every login). At scale on Postgres, a unique index on a varchar(255) is fine, but for very large user tables the index size matters. Consider hashing the email or using a citext column for case-insensitive lookups without `mode: 'insensitive'` (which the LDAP sync path may use).
suggested_fix: Verify via `EXPLAIN ANALYZE` that login queries hit the unique index. If they don't, add `@@index([email])` explicitly. Consider `lower(email)` index for case-insensitive login.

title: No composite indexes for common activity feed queries
description: Activity (line 392) has `@@index([projectId, createdAt])` (line 413), `@@index([projectId, isArchived])` (line 414), `@@index([subjectType, subjectId])` (line 415), and `@@index([userId, createdAt])` (line 416). Good coverage. But: the 'unread notifications' path queries `WHERE userId = ? AND read = false` — covered by DB-1's `@@index([userId, read])` (if kept) or `@@index([userId, read, createdAt])`. The polymorphic reference table is also missing a GIN index on the JSONB `reference` column for future 'find by subject.name' queries.
suggested_fix: Add a Prisma `Unsupported("gin")` raw index via migration: `CREATE INDEX activities_reference_gin ON activities USING GIN (reference);` (Prisma 7 supports this via `@@index([reference(ops: JsonbPathOps)], type: Gin)`). Optional; only if polymorphic JSON queries become a hot path.

title: Type.isMilestone is a free boolean, no check that only milestones have dueDate
description: Type (line 256) has `isMilestone Boolean @default(false)`. WorkPackage has `dueDate DateTime?` always, but only milestone types should meaningfully have a single due date (vs a range). No DB-level enforcement. Frontend can label a Task as milestone and it'll work, but WorkPackage.type.isMilestone isn't checked server-side when computing sprint burndown.
suggested_fix: Add a check constraint via raw migration: `ALTER TABLE work_packages ADD CONSTRAINT chk_milestone_due CHECK (NOT (typeId IN (SELECT id FROM types WHERE isMilestone = true) AND dueDate IS NULL));`. Skip if too aggressive — better to validate at the API layer.
